channel.title: Brazil Elections 2026
channel.description: Real-time monitoring of the 2026 Brazilian presidential elections.

item.title: Câmara terá “palavra final” sobre cassar deputados do PL, diz Motta
item.description: Chamber of Deputies President Hugo Motta stated that the House will have the 'final word' on the mandate loss of PL deputies convicted by the STF for embezzlement of parliamentary amendments, after the case becomes final and unappealable. The process will follow the CCJ review and then a plenary vote, respecting internal rules. Deputies Josimar Maranhãozinho, Pastor Gil, and Bosco Costa were unanimously convicted by the STF’s 1st Chamber for receiving bribes equivalent to 25% of the amendment funds directed to the municipality of São José de Ribamar.

item.title: Vanderlei Luxemburgo ganha apoio do Podemos para concorrer ao Senado de Tocantins
item.description: Vanderlei Luxemburgo received support from the Podemos party to run for the Senate in Tocantins in the 2026 elections. National party president Renata Abreu emphasized his preparation, history, and commitment to the state's development. Luxemburgo, who resides and owns property in Tocantins, previously attempted a Senate run in 2022 under the PSB but did not receive party backing at the time.

item.title: Brasil supera EUA em ranking mundial de democracia pela 1ª vez
item.description: Brazil surpassed the United States in the global democracy ranking for the first time in 2026, according to the V-Dem Institute's Democracy Report from the University of Gothenburg. The result reflects improvements in political participation, press freedom, and the rule of law in Brazil, while the U.S. showed setbacks in several democratic dimensions.

item.title: ONGs recorrem à ONU para evitar que governo brasileiro sancione PL Antifacção
item.description: Human rights NGOs Conectas Direitos Humanos and Rede Justiça Criminal appealed to the UN to urge the Lula government not to fully sanction the Anti-Faction Bill, arguing it threatens judicial guarantees and represents a human rights regression, including criminalizing families of inmates, ending prison assistance, and banning voting for pretrial detainees. They requested the UN pressure the government to conduct a social, legislative, and budgetary impact analysis before the expected sanction on March 19, 2026.

item.title: Governo libera R$ 1,3 bi para reconstrução em MG após chuva
item.description: The federal government has allocated R$ 1.3 billion for response and reconstruction efforts in areas affected by intense rainfall, primarily in Minas Gerais, through Provisional Measure No. 1.342/2026 signed by President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva. Funds will support social assistance (R$ 5 million for SUAS), housing (R$ 500 million for FAR, enabling 2,500 housing units), and credit access (R$ 300 million for FGO and R$ 500 million for climate mitigation and adaptation), coordinated by the Ministries of Social Development and Cities, with financial management by the Ministry of Finance.

item.title: PL fecha apoio a pré-candidatura de Sergio Moro, e senador deve ser palanque para Flávio no Paraná
item.description: The Liberal Party (PL) announced its support for Sergio Moro’s pre-candidacy for governor of Paraná, solving the issue of lacking a electoral base for Flávio Bolsonaro in the state. PL president Valdemar Costa Neto confirmed the support but noted Moro must decide whether to stay in União Brasil or join the PL. The move proceeds despite internal PP resistance in Paraná, which vetoed Moro in December, and Valdemar denied this constitutes a break with Ratinho Junior, who is running for president.

item.title: PL define apoio a Moro para o Governo do Paraná
item.description: The PL announced its support for Senator Sergio Moro's (União Brasil) candidacy for Governor of Paraná, aiming to create a platform for Flávio Bolsonaro's (PL) presidential campaign in the state. The decision followed a meeting between Moro and the party leadership, which leads in voter intention polls.

item.title: As conversas do primeiro encontro de Haddad com o núcleo duro de sua campanha em SP
item.description: Fernando Haddad met with the core team of his São Paulo gubernatorial campaign, including campaign strategist Otávio Antunes, deputy Jilmar Tatto, chief of staff Laio Moraes, and deputy Carlos Zarattini. Haddad acknowledged he had resisted the candidacy but now accepts the mission given by President Lula, insisting that belief in victory is essential. Attendees criticized Tarcísio de Freitas’s administration for 'privatizations and failures in education and tolls,' calling the current government 'very bad,' yet expressed confidence that defeating him is possible.

item.title: Eleições municipais na França: Macron alerta para alianças com partidos 'extremos'
item.description: President Emmanuel Macron warned against forming alliances with parties deemed 'extremes' in the upcoming municipal elections runoff in France. Although his Renaissance party had limited presence in the campaign, Macron stressed the need to avoid pacts with radical forces that threaten democratic stability.

item.title: Edinho Silva fala sobre polarização e eleições com trainees do Poder360
item.description: Edinho Silva, president of the Workers' Party (PT), discussed political polarization and the rise of conservative agendas linked to racist and xenophobic discourses during a lecture for Poder360 trainees on March 17, 2026. He also addressed economic challenges, internal party organization, and answered questions about electoral strategies for 2026, reinforcing his role as a key strategist in the governing coalition close to Lula.

item.title: Moraes manda PGR avaliar pedido da PF para compartilhar provas contra Eduardo
item.description: Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes has ordered the Attorney General's Office to respond within five days to a Federal Police request to use evidence from an investigation against Eduardo Bolsonaro in an internal disciplinary proceeding. The PF aims to strengthen its case regarding alleged administrative misconduct, including threats and exposure of agents, as well as abandonment of duty, given Eduardo's activities from the U.S. to push sanctions against Brazil to benefit his father, Jair Bolsonaro, who was convicted of leading a coup attempt.

item.title: Diretor da Aneel critica Enel por tentar suspender proces
item.description: The general director of Aneel, Sandoval Feitosa, criticized Enel São Paulo for attempting to suspend, via a mandamus, the process of caducity of its concession contract, which is under review by the regulatory agency. He stated that Aneel will appeal the judicial decision and that the company should focus on improving electricity service rather than spending on lawyers. The process, requested by federal, state, and municipal authorities following resident complaints about blackouts, had its viewing deadline extended to March 24.

item.title: 82% aprovam governo de Caiado em Goiás, diz Real Time Big Data
item.description: The Real Time Big Data poll shows 82% of Goiás voters approve of Governor Ronaldo Caiado's (PSD) administration, with 58% rating it as good or excellent. Health is the top priority for the next term (28%), followed by infrastructure (17%) and transportation (13%).

item.title: CPI do Crime aprova pedido para investigar beneficiários do Master
item.description: The Senate's Organized Crime CPI approved requests to investigate beneficiaries of the Banco Master fraud scheme, including summoning Martha Graeff and breaking bank and tax secrecy of Prime Aviation, but rejected the request to break Paulo Guedes' secrecy and summon Valdemar da Costa Neto. The commission also approved summoning Pedro Taques and heard former Central Bank supervisor Paulo Sérgio Neves de Souza.

item.title: Novos fatores jogam contra Lula, e cenário das pesquisas deve piorar, aponta especialista
item.description: Paraná Pesquisas surveys show Lula's government approval concentrated in the Northeast, with 57% in Ceará, while disapproval exceeds approval in the Southeast and South, reaching 61% in Paraná. Analyst Cristiano Noronha notes this reflects historical electoral patterns, with the government facing structural challenges outside its traditional strongholds, worsened by the Banco Master investigation, inflationary pressures from the Iran war, and potential trucker strikes. Despite efforts to build regional slates and push economic agendas, he expects the situation to remain largely unchanged in the short term with only marginal adjustments.

item.title: A dúvida continua, diz vice-presidente da CPMI do INSS sobre Lulinha
item.description: The INSS CPMI investigation into alleged billion-dollar pension fraud remains stalled due to lack of documentary evidence, particularly after Minister Flávio Dino blocked the lifting of secrecy on Lulinha, President Lula’s son. Deputy Duarte Jr., vice-president of the committee, stated that without access to documents, no definitive conclusion can be reached, and the extension of the inquiry hinges on a decision by Minister André Mendonça.

item.title: Presidente da CPI do INSS cobra investigação sobre empresa de Lulinha na Espanha
item.description: Senator Carlos Viana (Podemos-MG), president of the INSS Parliamentary Inquiry Committee (CPI), called for an investigation into the opening of a company in Spain by Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, son of President Lula. He stated this is another point needing clarification regarding the businessman’s activities. The statement was made on Wednesday, March 18, 2026.

item.title: Como Flávio Bolsonaro espera ficar mais popular e impulsionar nomes do PL antes do período eleitoral
item.description: Flávio Bolsonaro, senator and pre-candidate for presidency from the PL party, will appear in all regional party advertisements before the electoral period, alongside candidates for executive and legislative offices in every Brazilian state. The strategy aims to strengthen the link between selected candidates and the Bolsonaro clan while boosting Flávio's image during prime-time TV, even before the official electoral propaganda begins. Recordings are set to start next month.

item.title: O impacto para Lula da ameaça de greve dos caminhoneiros
item.description: The threat of a truckers' strike has resurfaced due to rising diesel prices, driven by the war in Iran, which is squeezing profit margins for drivers. Despite government actions — including PF investigations, Justice Ministry involvement, freight table enforcement, and ICMS reduction talks with Confaz — leaders say measures have been ineffective, raising fears of a repeat of the 2018 nationwide paralysis. The Lula administration worries about severe economic disruption and political damage, as prolonged strikes could worsen public approval and voting intentions ahead of the 2026 elections.

item.title: Hino Nacional obrigatório nas escolas públicas e privadas de SP? Entenda o que prevê nova lei
item.description: Governor Tarcísio de Freitas signed a law making the weekly playing of the National Anthem mandatory in all public and private elementary and high schools in São Paulo, preferably on Fridays. The law updates a 1990 regulation, keeping the anthem requirement but removing the flag-raising obligation, and mandates annual performance on the business day before September 7. Enforcement mechanisms and penalties for non-compliance remain unclear.

item.title: ‘Quadro de Bolsonaro é bom e tendência é melhorar’, diz médico
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro showed slight clinical improvement after being hospitalized for bronchopneumonia, with positive evolution in tomography scans and inflammatory markers, according to his doctor. His defense requested humanitarian house arrest before the STF, citing relevant clinical fragility, while he serves a 27-year-and-3-month sentence for leading a coup plot to overturn the 2022 presidential election results.

item.title: Você foi entrevistado sem saber sobre votar em Lula ou Flávio Bolsonaro. Quer saber como?
item.description: The text describes an unconventional electoral poll conducted without informing interviewees about their voting intention between Lula and Flávio Bolsonaro, using indirect methods to capture genuine preferences. This approach aims to avoid response biases common in traditional polls, revealing hidden trends in the electorate. The innovative methodology raises questions about the accuracy and ethics of political polling in Brazil.

item.title: Zema deixa governo com indefinição sobre que cargo vai disputar e sem transferir votos para vice
item.description: Governor Romeu Zema (Novo) will leave office in Minas Gerais on Sunday, March 22, without successfully transferring significant support to his running mate, Mateus Simões (PSD), who remains weak in the polls. Although Zema publicly states he will run for president in 2026, behind the scenes he is considered a potential vice-presidential candidate on a right-wing ticket, possibly with Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ). Polls show Cleitinho (Republicanos) leading the race for governor, while Simões trails between 9% and 19%, highlighting Zema’s limited ability to transfer electoral strength.

item.title: Governo vai barrar empresas que descumprem tabela do frete para evitar greve de caminhoneiros
item.description: The Lula government announced measures to enforce the minimum freight rate table, expanding electronic monitoring by the ANTT and creating a mechanism to bar repeat offenders from hiring transport services. The initiative aims to prevent a national truckers' strike by ensuring drivers' income and safety, as fines alone have failed to deter recurring violations of the freight table.

item.title: Ibovespa cai com expectativa por decisões de juros no Brasil e nos EUA
item.description: The Ibovespa opened lower on Wednesday (18) at 180,612 points, reflecting market focus on upcoming interest rate decisions by the Federal Reserve and Brazil's Central Bank. While the Fed is expected to hold rates steady, analysts anticipate the start of a Selic cutting cycle in Brazil. Major banks led losses, while some retailers gained. Internationally, Iran-Israel tensions rose after Iran's security chief was killed, but oil prices fell due to an Iraq-Kurdistan agreement to resume exports via Turkey's Ceyhan port.

item.title: Lula e Haddad criticaram alta dos combustíveis em 2021 e 2022
item.description: In 2021 and 2022, Lula and Haddad publicly criticized rising fuel prices in Brazil, blaming the federal government and Petrobras’ international parity pricing policy. In 2026, the Lula administration revived the debate by issuing MP 1.340, which imposed a 12% tax on oil exports to capture extra gains and curb domestic diesel prices, with Haddad stating the measure is temporary and fair amid global conflict.

item.title: CPI do Crime Organizado rejeita quebrar o sigilo de Paulo Guedes
item.description: The Senate's Organized Crime CPI rejected breaking the banking and fiscal secrecy of former Minister Paulo Guedes by a 6-2 vote, deeming the request outside its scope. It also denied summoning Valdemar Costa Neto but approved hearing influencer Martha Graeff, ex-fiancée of Daniel Vorcaro, over alleged contacts with Supreme Court Minister Alexandre de Moraes.

item.title: Crusoé: O possível elo entre Lulinha e o escândalo do INSS
item.description: The defense of Fábio Luís Lula da Silva, known as Lulinha, admitted he traveled to Portugal with Antônio Camilo, nicknamed 'Careca do INSS', with expenses covered by the latter, but denied any involvement of the president's eldest son in the INSS scandal. The case raises suspicions about potential links between Lula’s son and irregularities in Brazil’s National Institute of Social Security.

item.title: Visita ao Poder360: Edinho Silva
item.description: PT President Edinho Silva visited the Poder360 headquarters in Brasília on March 17, 2026, delivering a lecture to the second cohort of the digital outlet’s Journalism Trainees Program. He discussed political polarization, the 2026 elections, and Brazil’s economic outlook, while also answering questions about the current political context.

item.title: Senadora do MA diz ser vítima de complô contra sua candidatura à reeleição
item.description: Senator Eliziane Gama (PSD-MA) denounced in a Senate floor speech the existence of a political conspiracy in Maranhão aimed at blocking her re-election bid. She claims there are internal and external efforts to remove her from the 2026 electoral race. The statement was delivered on Tuesday, March 17, 2026.

item.title: Terras raras: EUA assina acordo com Goiás e aguarda resposta de Lula. Vídeo
item.description: The governor of Goiás, Ronaldo Caiado (PSD), signed a memorandum of understanding with the U.S. government on rare earths exploration in the state during an event in São Paulo. U.S. Chargé d’Affaires Gabriel Escobar said a federal proposal has been made and is under discussion, but they are still awaiting a response from the Lula administration. Caiado emphasized that the agreement grants Goiás priority in exploration, mineral mapping, and collaboration with U.S. universities to develop critical mineral separation technology.

item.title: A manobra de Motta para ajudar PT na disputa por vaga no TCU
item.description: House Speaker Hugo Motta is allegedly delaying the installation of the CFT to avoid hearings for TCU candidates and boost the chances of Odair Cunha, the PT-aligned nominee. The move aims to fulfill an agreement with the PT and improve Cunha's prospects for a seat on the TCU, despite opposition and Centrao fielding alternative candidates. Motta seeks to time the election for after the April partisan window closes, when committee amendments flow, enhancing his ability to influence lawmakers' support.

item.title: Petistas fazem ofensiva contra André Mendonça após decisão no STF
item.description: PT leaders and left-wing influencers accuse Supreme Court Justice André Mendonça of protecting Senator Flávio Bolsonaro by blocking congressional access to a secure room holding CPMI-INSS documents related to Daniel Vorcaro. They claim the decision followed the appearance of Bolsonaro’s name in Vorcaro’s contact list and suggest a 'shield' also surrounds former Central Bank chief Roberto Campos Neto. Deputies Lindbergh Farias (PT-RJ) and Rogério Correia (PT-MG) argued the move protects an alleged 'moral and financial orgy' in Vorcaro’s data.

item.title: Em conversa com Flávio Bolsonaro, Moraes diz entender relação entre pai e filho
item.description: Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) met with Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes to discuss the health of former President Jair Bolsonaro, who is hospitalized with bilateral bacterial pneumonia. Flávio emphasized the life-threatening risk due to lack of nighttime medical supervision in prison and requested a reassessment of the house arrest request. Moraes said he understands the father-son relationship in cases involving public figures and stated he will evaluate the petition.

item.title: Ministros do STF defendem prisão domiciliar para Bolsonaro e alertam para aumento do desgaste da Corte
item.description: STF ministers privately advocate for house arrest for former President Jair Bolsonaro due to new health issues following his imprisonment, warning that continued detention could increase institutional wear on the Court. After a meeting with Senator Flávio Bolsonaro and lawyer Paulo Bueno, ministers urged Alexandre de Moraes to grant house arrest to reduce political and institutional tensions surrounding the case.

item.title: OAB cita preocupação com novo imposto para exportação de petróleo
item.description: The OAB-RJ expressed concern over MP 1.340/2026, which imposes a 12% tax on crude oil exports, warning of judicial risks, regulatory uncertainty, and reduced investment appeal. The measure, enacted by Lula, is challenged by major oil firms (Shell, TotalEnergies, PetroRio, Repsol) for allegedly violating the principle of tax anteriority, while Petrobras supports it as a tool to capture extraordinary profits. Precedents from 2023 indicate the tax may be deemed primarily revenue-driven, undermining its legal justification.

item.title: Tarcísio vai ao STF por prisão domiciliar de Bolsonaro
item.description: São Paulo Governor Tarcísio de Freitas (Republicanos) is set to meet with Supreme Federal Court (STF) ministers in Brasília on Wednesday, the 18th, to advocate for house arrest for former President Jair Bolsonaro. The move is part of a broader political and legal effort to alter Bolsonaro's current incarceration status following his STF conviction.

item.title: Crusoé: Realtime aponta Caiado à frente de Lula (em Goiás)
item.description: A Realtime Big Data poll released on Wednesday, the 18th, shows Goiás Governor Ronaldo Caiado (PSD) leading President Lula (PT) and Flávio Bolsonaro (PL) in 2026 election voting intentions in Goiás, with 36% versus Lula’s 29%. The survey was conducted in the state Caiado governs, reflecting strong local support for the PSD candidate. Flávio Bolsonaro ranks third, though his exact percentage was not specified in the excerpt.

item.title: Lula aciona PF contra aumentos do diesel preocupado com reflexos negativos no ano eleitoral
item.description: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva has activated the Federal Police and the National Consumer Secretariat to investigate alleged diesel cartels, after intelligence indicated possible price-fixing using old stock. He is concerned about the inflationary impact of rising diesel prices in an election year, which could undermine his approval ratings and force the Central Bank to keep interest rates high. Meanwhile, Senator Flávio Bolsonaro (PL-RJ) is gaining traction as an opposition presidential candidate, capitalizing on the government's declining popularity.

item.title: Você sabe o que são direitos políticos? Confira no Glossário
item.description: Political rights are the set of entitlements that ensure full citizenship, enabling voting, running for elected office, appointment to public positions, participation in plebiscites and referendums, as well as popular initiatives and popular actions. Enjoying these rights is essential for voter registration and holding any public office, including non-elective ones. The TSE’s Electoral Glossary explains over 300 terms of electoral law, including historical context and bibliographical references on the evolution of Brazil’s electoral process.

item.title: O prazo esperado no PL para que Moraes conceda prisão domiciliar a Bolsonaro
item.description: Former President Jair Bolsonaro, hospitalized in the ICU at DF Star hospital due to bilateral bacterial bronchopneumonia, is not expected to be granted house arrest soon, according to PL party leaders. They state that Supreme Court Justice Alexandre de Moraes is likely to approve such a request only between June and July, ahead of the August party conventions. The timing aims to prevent Bolsonaro's imprisonment from becoming a rallying point of victimhood in Flávio Bolsonaro’s electoral campaign, thereby reducing judicial references during the election.

item.title: Lula quer Toffoli fora do STF
item.description: President Lula is seeking the removal of Justice Dias Toffoli from the Supreme Federal Court (STF) as part of a quiet strategy launched before the Banco Master scandal dominated national attention. Described as a 'cover-up operation,' the move aims not only to oust Toffoli but also to reshape power dynamics within the Judiciary, with broader political implications.

item.title: Governo diz que vai aumentar fiscalização contra alta dos combustíveis
item.description: The Justice Minister announced intensified monitoring against abusive fuel price increases, with a PF investigation and joint operations involving ANP, Senacon, Procons, and other agencies across 9 states and the Federal District. ANP inspected 42 gas stations and one distributor in 22 cities, with fines ranging from R$50,000 to R$500 million depending on severity. The government also unveiled a diesel relief package including tax cuts and subsidies, estimated to cost R$30 billion in public finances by end-2026.

item.title: JOTA Principal: Governo é pego de surpresa com ameaça de greve de caminhoneiros
item.description: The Lula government was caught off guard by the threat of a truckers' strike scheduled for today or tomorrow, driven by non-compliance with the minimum freight floor, lack of toll reimbursement, and rising diesel prices. Emergency articulations include activating Guilherme Boulos to negotiate with the category and Casa Civil studying measures to reduce tensions, while the opposition prepares to politically exploit the episode. The movement's national or regional scope remains unclear, but it has already triggered a red alert at the Planalto in an election year.

item.title: Deputado & Apucarana
item.description: Federal deputy Sérgio de Souza (MDB) previously garnered 5,000 votes in Apucarana with the backing of former mayor Beto Preto (PSD), but may lose this support in the 2026 elections if the former deputy fails to maintain local alliances. His electoral base in the city is vulnerable due to potential lack of regional political articulation.

item.title: Estado de saúde pode fazer Bolsonaro ir para prisão domiciliar? Veja o que diz especialista
item.description: Bolsonaro's defense filed a request for humanitarian house arrest at the STF, citing health concerns. The former president was sentenced to 27 years and 3 months for his role in the 2022 coup plot. The request aims to avoid incarceration in a closed regime.

item.title: Haddad se reúne nesta quarta com estados para falar sobre combustíveis; Lula quer redução do ICMS estadual
item.description: Finance Minister Fernando Haddad said the government will present a proposal at the Confaz meeting regarding the ICMS on diesel, without disclosing details. He noted that enforcement actions against organized crime in the fuel sector are boosting ICMS revenue without raising rates, while governors oppose reducing the tax, citing harm to public finance funding and lack of pass-through to consumers.

item.title: Plano climático do Brasil aposta no fim do desmatamento e prevê alta nas emissões de energia até 2035
item.description: Brazil unveiled its updated national climate plan, centering deforestation reduction as the core strategy since nearly half of emissions come from land-use change. Despite having one of the world’s cleanest energy matrices, the plan projects a rise in energy-sector emissions of 1% to 44% by 2035, relying on deforestation cuts to offset this increase. The country reaffirms its Paris Agreement commitments: cutting emissions by 59–67% by 2035 (vs. 2005) and reaching carbon neutrality by 2050.

item.title: A acirrada corrida ao Senado pelo Amazonas, segundo pesquisa AtlasIntel
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll shows a tight race for the two Senate seats from Amazonas in 2026. Captain Alberto Neto (PL) leads with 24%, followed by Eduardo Braga (MDB) at 19.3%, Plínio Valério (PSDB) at 17.4%, and Marcelo Ramos (PT) at 15.3%. With a margin of error of three percentage points, the leading candidates are technically tied.

item.title: Lula é desaprovado por 51% e aprovado por 40% no AM, diz pesquisa
item.description: An AtlasIntel poll released on March 18, 2026, shows 51% of Amazonas voters disapprove of President Lula’s government, while 40% approve. In the federal government evaluation, 49% rate the administration as bad or terrible, 39% as good or excellent, and 11% as regular. The survey interviewed 1,138 people aged 16 or older between March 11 and 15, with a 95% confidence level and a 3-point margin of error, registered with the TSE under number AM-06921/2026.
